creator: ino-news@spotteswoode.dnsalias.org
text:
[updated version of patch]

> clemens fischer:

> I have the following patch implementing a starting point for hooks.
> It provides what I need most: named logfiles.  The changes are meant
> to be non-intrusive and safe.  If the user doesn't supply hooks or
> they don't work, portmaster doesn't change its behaviour.  The patch
> is appended.  My hooks file looks like this:

These hooks let me name and save the log file and output the time period
an update took:

------ 8< -----
__user_logdir="/var/pkgtools/"
__user_category=""
__user_portname=""
mk_log_filename()
{
    local prefix="${1:-port_log}"
    local pid="${2}"
    local portdir="${3}"
    __user_category="${portdir%/*}"
    __user_portname="${portdir#*/}"
    echo "${__user_logdir}/${__user_category}__${__user_portname}.txt"
    return 0
}
dispose_log_file()
{
    return 0
}

__user_starttime=0
init_hook()
{
    __user_starttime="$(date '+%s')"
    echo "${0##*/} start time $(date -r ${__user_starttime} '+%H:%M:%S')."
    return 0
}
exit_hook()
{
    local duration_sum="$(($(date '+%s') - ${__user_starttime}))"
    /root/bin/beep
    echo "${0##*/} finished ${1:+code ${1} }after $(date -ur ${duration_sum} '+%H:%M:%S')."
    return 0
}
------ 8< -----

The following patch to portmaster(8) checks for and invokes user hooks,
and it checks files created by the user for validity.


--- /l/sbin/portmaster	2008-08-03 00:26:38.000000000 +0200
+++ /root/bin/portmaster.sh	2008-12-24 13:39:39.000000000 +0100
@@ -3,10 +3,54 @@
 # Copyright (c) 2005-2008 Douglas Barton, All rights reserved
 # Please see detailed copyright below
 
 trap trap_exit INT
 
+# Functions used for implementing user-hooks
+
+# Check for user-defined function
+function_defined()
+{
+    local name="${1}"
+    local typ="$(type "${name}" 2>/dev/null || echo '__nothing__')"
+    local ex=1
+    case ".${typ}" in
+        .__nothing__) ex=1;;
+        *is*/*) ex=1;;
+        *'an alias'*) ex=1;;
+        *'shell function'*) ex=0;;
+        *'shell builtin'*) ex=1;;
+        *) ex=1;;
+    esac
+    return $ex
+}
+
+# Try to make sure a filename is reasonably safe to use
+check_filename()
+{
+    local name="${1}"
+    local ex=1
+    case ".${name}" in
+        */boot/*|*/bin/*|*/sbin/*|*/lib/*|*/libexec/*) ex=1;;
+        ./*/*) ex=0;;
+        *) ex=1;;
+    esac
+    [ $ex -ne 0 ] && return 1
+    [ -e "${name}" ] || return 0
+    [ -x "${name}" -o -d "${name}" -o -p "${name}" ] && ex=1
+    [ -L "${name}" -o -S "${name}" ] && ex=1
+    [ -O "${name}" -o -G "${name}" ] || ex=1
+    return $ex
+}
+
+# Get user-hooks.  If put into an RC file, they do not work in children.
+# Read global hooks file first
+[ -r /etc/portmaster-hooks ] && . /etc/portmaster-hooks
+[ -r /usr/local/etc/portmaster-hooks ] && . /usr/local/etc/portmaster-hooks
+# Read local hooks next
+[ -r "$HOME/.portmaster-hooks" ] && . $HOME/.portmaster-hooks
+
 # Initialize crucial values for the parent, and export them for the children
 if [ -z "$PARENT_PID" ]; then
 	PARENT_PID=$$
 	: ${TMPDIR:=/tmp}
 	UPGRADE_TOOL=portmaster
@@ -31,10 +75,12 @@
 	set +o allexport
 
 	# If we are already root, unset this to avoid potential conflict
 	euid=`ps -o uid $$` ; euid=${euid##* }
 	[ "$euid" -eq 0 ] && unset PM_SU_CMD
+
+    function_defined "init_hook" && init_hook
 fi
 
 umask 022
 
 #=============== Begin functions we always want to have ===============
@@ -78,10 +124,11 @@
 			n=$(( $n + 1 ))
 			kill_bad_children
 		done
 		if [ -n "$HIDE_BUILD" ]; then
 			local logs file
+            # FIXME! -ino: path hardcoded
 			logs=`echo ${TMPDIR}/port_log-${PARENT_PID}-*`
 			case "$logs" in *\*) unset logs ;; esac
 			if [ -n "$logs" ]; then
 				echo ''
 				echo "===>>> Build/Install logs available:"
@@ -154,10 +201,12 @@
 			pm_rmdir_s $pbu
 		fi
 
 		for f in ${TMPDIR}/f-${PARENT_PID}-*; do pm_unlink $f ; done
 
+        function_defined "exit_hook" && exit_hook ${1:-0}
+
 		case "$DISPLAY_LIST" in
 		*' '*)	if [ -z "$UNATTENDED" -a -n "$TRAP" ]; then
 	echo "===>>> There are messages from installed ports to display,"
 	echo "       but first take a moment to review the error messages"
 	echo -n "       above.  Then press Enter when ready to proceed. "
@@ -2088,10 +2137,13 @@
 	fi
 done
 unset sleep
 
 if [ -n "$HIDE_BUILD" ]; then
+    function_defined "mk_log_filename" &&
+	port_log=`mk_log_filename port_log ${PARENT_PID} ${portdir}`
+    check_filename "${port_log}" ||
 	port_log=`mktemp -t port_log-${PARENT_PID}-${portdir#*/}`
 	port_log_args=">> $port_log 2>&1"
 	echo "===>>> Logging build to $port_log"
 fi
 
@@ -2312,11 +2364,15 @@
 
 INSTALLED_LIST="${INSTALLED_LIST}\t${ilist}\n"
 [ -e "$pdb/$new_port/+DISPLAY" ] && DISPLAY_LIST="${DISPLAY_LIST}$new_port "
 CUR_DEPS="${CUR_DEPS}${new_port}:${portdir}:"
 
-[ -n "$HIDE_BUILD" -a -n "$port_log" ] && pm_unlink $port_log
+if [ -n "$HIDE_BUILD" -a -n "$port_log" ]; then
+    function_defined "dispose_log_file" &&
+    dispose_log_file $port_log ||
+    pm_unlink $port_log
+fi
 
 [ -n "$URB_YES" -o -n "$UPDATE_REQ_BYS" ] &&
 	URB_DONE_LIST="${URB_DONE_LIST}${new_port}:"
 [ -n "$URB_YES" -o -n "$UPDATE_REQ_BYS" -o -n "$PM_FORCE" ] &&
 	$PM_SU_CMD touch $pdb/$new_port/PM_UPGRADE_DONE_FLAG
